findings.title: SMB Signing Not Required
findings.description: SMB2 security mode indicates signing is enabled but not required. In environments where NTLM relay conditions exist, this weakens protection against certain relay and man-in-the-middle scenarios.
findings.evidence: nmap smb2-security-mode reported: Message signing enabled but not required
findings.impact: If a relay path exists elsewhere in the network, attackers may be able to leverage SMB authentication flows more effectively than on hosts requiring signing.
findings.remediation: Require SMB signing on the host and through domain or local policy, then verify with smb2-security-mode or equivalent tooling.
findings.hardening: Reduce unnecessary SMB exposure to trusted admin segments only, disable legacy protocols, and monitor for NTLM relay-style behavior.

findings.title: Multiple Remote Administration Services Exposed
findings.description: The workstation exposes several remote administration and management services including RPC, NetBIOS/SMB, RDP, WinRM, and a likely AnyDesk service over TLS.
findings.evidence: Nmap detected open services on 135/tcp, 139/tcp, 445/tcp, 3389/tcp, 5985/tcp, and 7070/tcp with Windows/RDP/HTTPAPI/AnyDesk indicators.
findings.impact: This increases the attack surface for credential attacks, lateral movement, remote management abuse, and reconnaissance against an internal workstation.
findings.remediation: Limit exposed remote management services to those operationally required. Restrict access via host firewall and network segmentation to authorized admin systems only.
findings.hardening: Require VPN or jump-host access for administration, review AnyDesk necessity, and enforce strong authentication and monitoring on RDP/WinRM.

findings.title: RDP Reveals Domain and Host Metadata
findings.description: RDP NTLM information exposed the NetBIOS name, DNS hostname, domain name, and Windows build metadata during enumeration.
findings.evidence: rdp-ntlm-info returned NetBIOS_Computer_Name=G8, DNS_Computer_Name=G8.nctv360.com, DNS_Domain_Name=nctv360.com, Product_Version=10.0.26100.
findings.impact: This aids attacker reconnaissance and supports tailored credential attacks or environment mapping.
findings.remediation: Restrict RDP exposure to trusted administration sources and disable it if not needed.
findings.hardening: Place RDP behind VPN or bastion access, enforce NLA and MFA where possible, and monitor failed logons and RDP connection attempts.

findings.title: No Confirmed Legacy SMB Vulnerabilities in Safe NSE Pass
findings.description: A safe NSE vulnerability pass did not confirm MS10-054 or identify a clear exploitable legacy SMB issue. Some vuln scripts failed negotiation rather than proving exposure.
findings.evidence: smb-vuln-ms10-054: false; smb-vuln-ms10-061 and samba-vuln-cve-2012-1182 did not negotiate successfully.
findings.impact: This reduces confidence in obvious legacy SMB exposure but does not prove the host is hardened against other auth or configuration issues.
findings.remediation: Keep SMB fully patched and confirm hardening with authenticated Windows security review if needed.
findings.hardening: Continue patch management, restrict SMB to trusted peers, and validate with authenticated assessment during a deeper review.

enhancements.recommendation: Restrict workstation administration ports (RDP, WinRM, SMB, AnyDesk) to a dedicated admin subnet or jump host instead of leaving them broadly reachable on the LAN.
Review SMB signing policy, enforce least-privilege remote administration, and disable remote services that are not actively required.
Alert on repeated RDP, SMB, or WinRM authentication attempts and watch for abnormal remote management access patterns from non-admin systems.
